# zstack_gpu_devices (Data Source)

Fetches a list of GPU devices and their associated attributes from the ZStack environment.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `filter` (Block List) Filter resources based on any field in the schema. For example, to filter by vendor, use `name = "vendor"` and `values = ["NVIDIA"]`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--filter))
- `name` (String) Exact name for searching GPU devices.
- `name_pattern` (String) Pattern for fuzzy name search, similar to MySQL LIKE. Use % for multiple characters and _ for exactly one character.

### Read-Only

- `gpu_devices` (Attributes List) List of GPU devices matching the specified filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gpu_devices))

<a id="nestedblock--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the field to filter by (e.g., vendor, gpu_type, state).
- `values` (Set of String) Values to filter by. Multiple values will be treated as an OR condition.


<a id="nestedatt--gpu_devices"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gpu_devices`

Read-Only:

- `allocate_status` (String) Allocation status of the GPU (e.g., allocated, available).
- `description` (String) Description of the GPU device.
- `device` (String) GPU device model name.
- `device_id` (String) PCI device ID.
- `gpu_status` (String) Status of the GPU device.
- `gpu_type` (String) Type of the GPU (e.g., vGPU, passthrough).
- `host_uuid` (String) UUID of the host where the GPU is installed.
- `memory` (Number) GPU memory in bytes.
- `name` (String) Name of the GPU device.
- `pci_device_address` (String) PCI device address.
- `state` (String) State of the GPU device (Enabled, Disabled).
- `status` (String) Connection status of the GPU device.
- `type` (String) Device type.
- `uuid` (String) UUID of the GPU device.
- `vendor` (String) GPU vendor name (e.g., NVIDIA).
- `vendor_id` (String) PCI vendor ID.
- `vm_instance_uuid` (String) UUID of the VM instance the GPU is attached to, if any.
